title: Exercises: Classical Fourier Transform
description: Practice computing and understanding the DFT

exercises.title: Implement DFT
exercises.description: Implement the Discrete Fourier Transform from scratch and verify it matches NumPy's FFT.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def my_dft(x):
    """Compute DFT of sequence x."""
    N = len(x)
    X = np.zeros(N, dtype=complex)
    # Your code here
    return X

# Test
signal = [1, 2, 3, 4]
my_result = my_dft(signal)
np_result = np.fft.fft(signal)
print('Match:', np.allclose(my_result, np_result))
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def my_dft(x):
    """Compute DFT of sequence x."""
    N = len(x)
    X = np.zeros(N, dtype=complex)
    for k in range(N):
        for n in range(N):
            X[k] += x[n] * np.exp(-2j * np.pi * k * n / N)
    return X

# Test
signal = [1, 2, 3, 4]
my_result = my_dft(signal)
np_result = np.fft.fft(signal)
print('My DFT:', np.round(my_result, 2))
print('NumPy FFT:', np.round(np_result, 2))
print('Match:', np.allclose(my_result, np_result))
hints: Use nested loops: outer for k, inner for n

exercises.title: DFT Matrix
exercises.description: Create the DFT matrix and use matrix multiplication to compute the DFT.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def dft_matrix(N):
    """Create N×N DFT matrix."""
    # Your code here
    pass

F = dft_matrix(4)
print('DFT Matrix shape:', F.shape)
print('F @ [1,2,3,4] =', F @ np.array([1,2,3,4]))
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def dft_matrix(N):
    """Create N×N DFT matrix."""
    omega = np.exp(-2j * np.pi / N)
    indices = np.arange(N)
    return omega ** np.outer(indices, indices)

F = dft_matrix(4)
print('DFT Matrix:')
print(np.round(F, 2))
print('\nF @ [1,2,3,4] =', np.round(F @ np.array([1,2,3,4]), 2))
print('NumPy FFT:', np.round(np.fft.fft([1,2,3,4]), 2))

exercises.title: Inverse DFT
exercises.description: Implement the inverse DFT and verify it recovers the original signal.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def inverse_dft(X):
    """Compute inverse DFT."""
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test round-trip
original = [1, 2, 3, 4]
spectrum = np.fft.fft(original)
recovered = inverse_dft(spectrum)
print('Recovered:', np.round(recovered.real, 2))
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def inverse_dft(X):
    """Compute inverse DFT."""
    N = len(X)
    x = np.zeros(N, dtype=complex)
    for n in range(N):
        for k in range(N):
            x[n] += X[k] * np.exp(2j * np.pi * k * n / N)
    return x / N

# Test round-trip
original = [1, 2, 3, 4]
spectrum = np.fft.fft(original)
recovered = inverse_dft(spectrum)
print('Original:', original)
print('Recovered:', np.round(recovered.real, 2))
print('Match:', np.allclose(original, recovered))
hints: Divide by N at the end
